mnemonics: ELI (Ethereum Layer Infrastructure) Explains Gas Costs: EVM Executes, L2 Lowers, Clients Connect
keyPoints: The Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) is the global, decentralized computer that processes all smart contract transactions.
Gas is the unit that measures the computational effort required to execute operations, paid for in ETH.
Clients (like Geth or Nethermind) are software that allows a node to participate in the network by verifying transactions and blocks.
commonMistakes: Confusing Ether (ETH the currency) with Gas (the unit of computational effort paid for in ETH).
Believing Layer 2 solutions (like Arbitrum or Optimism) are separate blockchains; they are execution layers that settle finality on Ethereum Mainnet.
